text.title: API Rate Limiting Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped api-rate-limiting work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Corporate office hits limit because 200 employees share one NAT IP; authenticated abuse from one bad account ignored.
antipattern.fix: Switch to AUTH-derived key; keep IP as a secondary signal for unauthenticated routes. See rule key-from-auth.

antipattern.symptom: Client retries every 100ms after 429; backend sees synthesised DoS.
antipattern.fix: Set Retry-After to seconds until next available slot; document in error catalogue. See rule 429-with-retry-after.

antipattern.symptom: Free-tier users blocked at 60/min; paid partner with 1000-RPS quota cannot exceed the same 60/min.
antipattern.root-cause: One quota policy for all callers.
antipattern.fix: Define per-tier table; assign tier on auth resolve. See rule per-tier-quotas.

antipattern.symptom: p95 latency jumps; SRE digs for an hour before noticing rate-limit middleware is rejecting 8% of traffic.
antipattern.fix: Add counters for hits / rejects + histogram for limiter latency; alert on reject_rate > 1%. See rule observable-eviction.
